quoted_tweet.text: DTCC tokenized securities go live for partial trading july 2026 with full production october. USDC is the settlement currency. if 1% of daily US equity volume routes through tokenized rails that's $29B daily in USDC settlement demand. current USDC transfer volume across all use cases is ~$280B monthly. one use case would 3x total USDC velocity. circle, chainlink, securitize, backpack all confirmed in the working group alongside blackrock, JPM, goldman, citadel. securitize got FINRA approval for atomic settlement may 4. chainlink provides proof of reserve and data feeds across the entire asset lifecycle. the value accrues to the settlement layer and oracle layer, not to platform tokens with no fee capture. already posted about ondo's 108x P/S disconnect. apply that lens to every RWA token. the chokepoints are USDC and LINK. every tokenized equity trade must settle in one and price through the other.

kept.text:
SECURITIZE BECOMES FIRST REGULAR BROKER-DEALER APPROVED TO CUSTODY TOKENIZED SECURITIES

@Securitize received FINRA approval to expand its broker-dealer activities, claiming the first regular (non-special-purpose) broker-dealer authorization to custody tokenized securities and offering the first full-stack onchain IPO infrastructure.

The approval enables atomic swaps and clearing/settlement between tokenized securities and stablecoins onchain in a single transaction. Securitize Markets can now also act as underwriter or selling group participant for tokenized securities offerings.

Real-time, onchain settlement within a regulated broker-dealer is now possible. The structure compresses traditional T+1 cycles into a single atomic exchange.

President Brett Redfearn (@Observatory13) said the case for new and existing publicly traded companies to tokenize stock "continues to get more compelling."

kept.text:
#Crypto Weekly News

• StanChart + BlackRock + OKX launch BUIDL collateral framework
• Securitize + CPU token-equities
• Meta pays in USDC (Stripe)
• Rayls launch
• EigenCloud Acquisition
• Polymarket + Chainalysis vs insiders
• Trump “Project Freedom”

Next:
• HederaCon 2026 May 4
• Consensus Miami May 5-7
• ETHPrague May 8-10

BUIDL collateral framework
Standard Chartered, BlackRock, and OKX launched a framework letting OKX’s institutional clients use BlackRock’s BUIDL tokenized U.S. Treasury fund as yield-bearing collateral for trading, lending, and borrowing. Assets stay in regulated off-exchange custody at Standard Chartered (the first time a G-SIB has enabled this) bridging TradFi liquidity straight into crypto markets.

Securitize + CPU token-equities
Securitize teamed with Computershare to let U.S.-listed companies issue tokenized equities as Issuer-Sponsored Tokens (ISTs) alongside traditional shares. This opens a compliant on-chain path for the $70T U.S. stock market without changing existing market structure.

Meta pays in USDC (Stripe)
Meta began paying select creators directly in USDC via Stripe on Solana and Polygon blockchains. The rollout started in Colombia and the Philippines, letting users receive earnings straight to wallets like MetaMask or Phantom and skip traditional cross-border fees and delays.

Rayls launch
Rayls officially launched its public mainnet on April 30 (an EVM Layer-1 built for banks and institutions). It delivers enterprise-grade speed, tokenized real-world yield, and direct on-chain liquidity rails for regulated finance.

EigenCloud Acquisition
Nebius agreed to acquire Eigen AI (strengthening its EigenCloud infrastructure) for ~$643M to boost AI inference and model optimization. The deal supercharges decentralized cloud capacity for Ethereum scaling and high-performance blockchain apps.

Polymarket + Chainalysis vs insiders
Polymarket partnered with Chainalysis to deploy real-time on-chain monitoring that detects and blocks insider trading and market manipulation. The new surveillance tools bring Wall Street-level integrity to crypto prediction markets.

Trump “Project Freedom”
Trump announced “Project Freedom,” a U.S. military operation to escort neutral cargo ships through the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing Iran tensions. The move triggered a risk-on rally that pushed Bitcoin above $80,000 while easing some oil-supply fears.

What’s the biggest catalyst for crypto this week: “Project Freedom” or Clarity Act finally getting traction (pushing Circle +20% intraday)?

Your thoughts 👇

profile_bio.description:
Daily Cross-Asset Journal | 9-mo Chain
95% Market Drivers, proved by @Grok

quoted_tweet.text:
#Crypto Weekly News

• Apple: ~26 fake wallets
• 120+ firms push CLARITY Act
• Litecoin 13-block reorg
• NY sued Coinbase/Gemini
• Stripe L1 “Tempo”
• SEC “tokenization exemption”
• Trump crypto event at Mar-a-Lago
• BIS stablecoin rules
• Binance AI Wallet

Next:
• BTC Conf Apr 27-29
• MegaETH + Rayls mainnet Apr 30
• SUI/EIGEN/OMNI/ENA unlocks May 1-2

Apple: ~26 fake wallets
Kaspersky uncovered 26 malicious “FakeWallet” apps in the Apple App Store (China region) that perfectly mimic MetaMask, Coinbase Wallet, Ledger, Trust Wallet, and others. They steal seed phrases and drain funds instantly. Apple is removing them now.

120+ firms push CLARITY Act
Over 120 crypto companies (Coinbase, Ripple, Kraken, Circle, a16z) sent a joint letter to the Senate Banking Committee demanding immediate markup on the CLARITY Act before the May deadline. The bill already passed the House and would create clear rules separating commodities from securities.

Litecoin 13-block reorg
Litecoin suffered a DoS attack exploiting a zero-day in its MWEB protocol, forcing a 13-block reorganization (32 minutes of chain history). The fix was deployed quickly with no major user losses. It highlights ongoing risks for layer-1 upgrades and bridges.

NY sued Coinbase/Gemini
New York AG Letitia James sued Coinbase and Gemini, claiming their prediction-market platforms are unlicensed illegal gambling operations. The suits seek injunctions and potentially billions in fines. This is a major test case for the multi-billion-dollar prediction market sector.

Stripe L1 “Tempo”
Stripe (with Paradigm) launched Tempo. It’s dedicated payments Layer-1. It introduces the Machine Payments Protocol so AI agents can autonomously pay across any rail. Public RPCs are now open for developers. DoorDash already using it.

SEC “tokenization exemption”
The SEC confirmed tokenized real-world assets are still securities but introduced a new innovation exemption/sandbox for 2026. Eligible projects get temporary relief and lighter registration, speeding up institutional RWA adoption.

Trump crypto event at Mar-a-Lago
President Trump hosted an exclusive crypto conference at Mar-a-Lago for the top 297 holders of his TRUMP meme coin (top 29 got private access). The event mixed industry talks with personal branding and politics.

BIS stablecoin rules
The Bank for International Settlements called for global coordination on stablecoin regulation to prevent fragmentation, runs, monetary-policy erosion, and risks to developing economies. Stablecoins are now too large for patchwork national rules.

Binance AI Wallet
Binance launched its Agentic AI Wallet. A keyless sub-account where AI agents can autonomously trade and manage assets within user-set limits. It’s designed to make DeFi safe and easy for non-technical users.

Which story do you think will have the biggest impact this week: Clarity Act, SEC exemption or Binance AI Agentic Wallet?

Share your thoughts below 👇

TRON was happy to participate in the #Bitcoin 2026 conference, held April 27-29 at The Venetian in Las Vegas. As part of its presence, TRON hosted TRON Whale Night alongside @Securitize on April 27, with @MetaMask, Digital Sovereignty Alliance (@DSAForg), and @BAI_AGI serving as co-hosts.

Held at OMNIA Nightclub on the Las Vegas Strip, TRON Whale Night brought together hundreds of participants, including leading institutions, builders, developers, and industry stakeholders.

More details from @newsfile_corp👇
https://t.co/koXBUUzDM4

profile_bio.description: TRON is dedicated to building the infrastructure for a decentralized internet. 🌐

kept.text:
All eyes are on Omnia tonight as #TRON Whale Night kicks off, one of the events that clearly reflects TRON’s strong presence across crypto and institutional Web3 🪩

What stands out is that this is not just another gathering. It brings TRON together with major names like Securitize, MetaMask, DSA, and https://t.co/e5e5tKPW8s, which sends a clear signal that the future of blockchain is now closely connected to institutional finance, wallets, infrastructure, and AI.

TRON’s presence at events like this matters because it opens the door for deeper conversations around the role of high-usage networks in the next phase, especially as stablecoin adoption expands, payment solutions gain more attention, user experience improves, and Web3 becomes more connected to real-world applications.

⭐️ The strength of any ecosystem is not shown only by on-chain numbers, but also by its ability to bring influential players to the same table and build relationships that support long-term growth 🚀

A big night for TRON, and a clear message that the momentum is moving across multiple fronts.

#TRONEcoStar @justinsuntron @trondao

USDC is the settlement currency. if 1% of daily US equity volume routes through tokenized rails that's $29B daily in USDC settlement demand. current USDC transfer volume across all use cases is ~$280B monthly. one use case would 3x total USDC velocity. circle, chainlink, securitize, backpack all confirmed in the working group alongside blackrock, JPM, goldman, citadel. securitize got FINRA approval for atomic settlement may 4. chainlink provides proof of reserve and data feeds across the entire asset lifecycle. the value accrues to the settlement layer and oracle layer, not to platform tokens with no fee capture. already posted about ondo's 108x P/S disconnect. apply that lens to every RWA token. the chokepoints are USDC and LINK. every tokenized equity trade must settle in one and price through the other.

tweet.text:
At ~$27B versus a ~$400T global market, RWAs still look small in scale, but the shift is structural: from static token issuance to assets embedded in systems where they can move, interact, and support broader financial strategies. 

What was once a narrative direction is increasingly resembling early-stage financial infrastructure rather than experimentation.

◢ From Representation to Function

The distinction between ownership and utility is becoming clearer. A token that passively earns yield is limited. The more important development is the ability to reuse that same position across multiple functions, collateral, borrowing base, or liquidity source.

Infrastructure like Securitize’s Vault Registrar reflects this shift. It allows a single position to operate across layers while maintaining compliance requirements like identity tracking. That’s where tokenization begins to resemble a financial primitive rather than a static record.

◢ How Different Assets Are Evolving

Not all RWAs are progressing at the same pace. Their structure determines how easily they translate on-chain.

1. Tokenized Treasuries (27/30)
This segment has quietly become the foundation. Their characteristics, fungibility, continuous pricing, and legal clarity, fit naturally into digital systems.

Demand is converging from multiple sides:

▸ Stablecoin reserves: @tether and @circle hold over $139B in T-bills
▸ Institutional infrastructure: @GoldmanSachs and @BNYglobal are building tokenized money markets
▸ DeFi allocation: @sparkdotfi alone directed ~$2B through its Tokenization Grand Prix

They now represent ~67% of the RWA market, adding ~$9B+ recently. In Q1 2026, their yields exceeded @aave v3 $USDC/$USDT lending rates on 88 of 90 days, with 3.6x lower monthly volatility.

Key assets include $USDY and $OUSG (@OndoFinance), $BUIDL (@BlackRock), $USYC (@circle), and $BENJI (@FTI_US).

2. Commodities, Led by Gold (21/30)
Gold appears to be the first category to reach consistent product-market fit.

Market cap grew 289% from $1.4B to $5.5B, while trading volume reached $90.7B in Q1 2026, already exceeding all of 2025.

Liquidity is concentrated: roughly 90% sits in $XAUT (@tethergold) and $PAXG (@Paxos), with newer entries like $KAG (@KinesisMonetary) and $XAUM (@matrixdock) beginning to appear.

3. Equities (22/30)
The opportunity here is tied to access rather than liquidity. U.S. equities already clear over $300B daily; the challenge is regulatory integration.

So far, tokenized stocks have reached ~$0.5B in market cap with $15.1B in Q1 2026 volume. Demand is visible, @Tesla, @nvidia, and Circle lead, but still represents under 1% of traditional market activity.

4. RWA Perpetuals
Activity is increasingly moving into derivatives.

Volumes reached $524.8B in Q1 2026, already surpassing the $313B recorded across all of 2025. Platforms like @HyperliquidX (HIP-3) and @OstiumLabs are central to this shift, suggesting RWAs are following the same path as crypto, spot first, leverage next.

5. Tokenized ETFs
This segment reflects expansion rather than concentration.

At ~$0.3B, exposure is spread across products like the S&P 500, QQQ, and silver trusts. No single asset holds more than ~11%, indicating fragmentation rather than dominance.

6. Private Credit & Alternative Funds (17/30, 14/30)
These categories show the limits of tokenization.

Lower minimums (from millions to ~$10K) improve access, but underlying constraints, illiquidity and infrequent valuation, remain. The token cannot be more liquid than the asset itself.

◢ A Familiar Strategy, Now More Accessible

One of the more notable developments is how traditional strategies are being replicated.

The Treasury carry trade, once limited to institutions, is now possible on-chain. By depositing assets like $BUIDL or $USDY, borrowing stablecoins, and looping the position, users can increase exposure as long as borrowing costs remain below yield.

A $1,000 position scaling to ~$6,900 after ten loops illustrates the mechanics. The structure isn’t new; the accessibility is.

◢ The Emerging Structure

What’s forming is a three-layer system:

▸ Issuance: BlackRock, Ondo Finance
▸ Distribution: @binance, @krakenfx, @okx, @Bybit_Official driving most volume
▸ Trading: Increasingly centered in DeFi, particularly platforms like Hyperliquid

Liquidity, especially in gold and derivatives, is growing faster than market cap, suggesting usage is deepening before scale fully arrives.

◢ The broader pattern is gradual. RWAs are not expanding evenly across all assets. They’re taking hold first where structure, liquidity, and demand already align, starting with the simplest instruments and moving outward from there.

profile_bio.description: Web3 educator and trader. Simplifying blockchain and narratives. Opinions are my own.
